items.headline: Are perps swaps? A quick look at that CME suit: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: CME Group sued the CFTC on Thursday, alleging that the agency was wrong in how it approved Kalshi's first U.S. perpetual futures product.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Bank of England backs down on strict stablecoin holding limits, sets $50 billion issuance cap
items.subheadline: The U.K. central bank abandons retail holding limits for a 40-billion-pound aggregate cap and sweetens yield terms for token issuers ahead of a 2027 market launch.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: TradFi fund manager Baillie Gifford introduces Solana, Ethereum tokenized fund with BNY
items.subheadline: Baillie Gifford Enhanced Yield Fund (BAGEY) is denominated in dollars, and gives eligible investors access to an actively managed, short-duration portfolio of public corporate bonds.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Michael Saylor's MSTR boosted BTC and cash holdings
items.subheadline: The latest purchase increases Strategy's bitcoin treasury to 847,363 while strengthening its liquidity position through additional cash reserves.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Bitcoin miners near breakeven as network reacts more sharply to price swings: JPMorgan
items.subheadline: A growing share of miners are operating near breakeven levels, making hashrate and mining difficulty increasingly responsive to bitcoin's price movements, the bank said.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: U.S. senators urge Treasury not to leave states out of GENIUS Act stablecoin process
items.subheadline: Senators led by Republican Cynthia Lummis are insisting the Treasury Department ensure states get a process to prove their ability to supervise stablecoins.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Live: Bitcoin and ether ETFs lost $111 million combined as rate-cut hopes died
items.subheadline: Total crypto market value has held steady near $2.26 trillion since Tuesday, with the recovery losing momentum after the Fed killed rate-cut hopes and spot ETFs swung back to outflows.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Geopolitical relief meets the Warsh Fed: Crypto Week Ahead
items.subheadline: Your look at what's coming in the week starting June 22.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Fifth-generation Kanoo group member is moving a $6 trillion trade market onto blockchain rails
items.subheadline: Member of 135-year-old group is moving a $6 trillion trade market onto blockchain rails
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Mexican billionaire with 70% of his investment portfolio in bitcoin says it's better than real estate
items.subheadline: Ricardo Salinas Pliego, whose net worth is estimated at roughly $5 billion, is a hardcore bitcoin maximalist who told his wife to mortgage her house to buy bitcoin.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Solana news: Moody’s rolls out credit ratings onchain in tokenized asset push
items.subheadline: The ratings giant is embedding credit scores directly into blockchain-based securities, a move aimed at boosting institutional adoption.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Here is how Coinbase plan to survive the crypto winter by ditching its reliance on trading fees
items.subheadline: Analysts say the exchange's push into derivatives, payments and infrastructure broadens its growth story beyond crypto trading.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Alchemy's AI-driven AgentCard gains access to Visa payments network
items.subheadline: The AgentCard integration with Visa Intelligent Commerce allows AI agents built on models from any provider, including OpenAI or Anthropic, to effect commercial transactions.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: GoMining challenges Jack Dorsey's Square with a pure BTC payment rail
items.subheadline: GoMining unveiled a SDK and API for its BTC payment protocol GoBTC Pay, allowing merchants to accept bitcoin for everyday purchases.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: As BTC, ETH prices gain, derivatives signal skepticism over a sustained rally
items.subheadline: Analysts noted bitcoin is stuck between key support near $60,000 and resistance around $68,000, and a bearish chart pattern could send prices toward $54,000.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Algorand unveils roadmap for post-quantum security by end-2027
items.subheadline: The announcement reflects a growing recognition across the crypto industry that transitioning to quantum-resistant cryptography could take years, requiring changes not only to user wallets but also to core protocol infrastructure.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Foundation loses another key leader as Hsiao-Wei Wang resigns
items.subheadline: Wang's departure follows the resignation of fellow co-executive director Tomasz Stańczak and marks the latest in a string of high-profile exits at the EF.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Microsoft identifies malware 'worm' that hijacks crypto wallets, spreads through USB drives
items.subheadline: The software intercepts shortcut files and directs them to install a worm that harvests private keys from the Windows clipboard and inserts its own destination wallet addresses when it detects a transfer.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: AI is making crypto security cheaper, faster and harder to ignore
items.subheadline: As AI-powered security tools become cheaper, faster and more widely available, researchers said they could reshape what the crypto industry considers reasonable due diligence before deploying code, potentially altering expectations for developers and institutions.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum's biggest 'sandwich' bot drained of $7.5 million in ironic exploit
items.subheadline: Blockaid said an attacker tricked Jaredfromsubway.eth into approving fake trading routes, then used those approvals to drain WETH, USDC and USDT.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ether news: Ethereum could fund soon projects with up to 10% of staking rewards
items.subheadline: A new governance proposal would let validators redirect part of their staking income toward ecosystem funding, raising questions about coordination, incentives and who gets to decide where the money goes.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin developers look to remove old fee signal that leaks wallet clues
items.subheadline: A helpful feature of speeding up transactions has become redundant and a "fingerprint" for tracking. Developers now want to do away with it.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live updates: Strategy buys 520 more bitcoin and raises reserves to $1.4 billion
items.subheadline: Eased ETF selling and improving risk appetite are being offset by a firmer dollar and cautious institutional flows, leaving bitcoin range-bound, one analyst said.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Taiko halts Ethereum layer-2 network after a bridge exploit, token dives
items.subheadline: An attacker forged withdrawal proofs to drain about $1.7 million, the same type of flaw behind this year's biggest bridge hacks. Fast containment kept the damage small.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: Crypto PAC's $12 million Senate candidate, Barry Moore, wins Alabama GOP primary
items.subheadline: Crypto PAC poured $12 million into Barry Moore’s Alabama Senate bid, and it worked: the Trump loyalist crushed former Navy SEAL Jared Hudson in the GOP primary runoff, setting him up as the likely nominee in November.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto industry aghast at Illinois' new tax on holding or transferring digital assets in state budget
items.subheadline: The 0.2% tax on any business activity involving digital assets was added last-minute, and is unlikely to change, two people familiar with the matter said.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Kentucky targets prediction markets, puts Trump-aligned state in potential clash with president's team
items.subheadline: President Donald Trump has taken the stance that states have no business with firms like Kalshi and Polymarket, and now a staunch GOP state is defying that view.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: CME chief executive says company plans to sue CFTC after perpetual futures approval
items.subheadline: Kalshi's perpetual futures product did not meet the Dodd-Frank Act's definition of a "swap" and should not have been approved, Terrence Duffy said.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Malta's financial regulator explores bringing parts of DeFi under MiCA's orbit
items.subheadline: The MFSA is seeking feedback on whether decentralization should be assessed as a spectrum rather than a binary concept.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: U.S. agencies seek stablecoin customer-ID rules akin to banks in new GENIUS Act rule
items.subheadline: The Federal Reserve, Treasury and other regulators have issued a proposed rule that would set identification standards, and it's now open for public comments.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Ex-Celsius CEO Mashinsky gets U.S. CFTC ban in final resolution with regulator
items.subheadline: Alexander Mashinsky, the founder of failed crypto lender Celsius, had earlier been imprisoned for fraud and is now formally banned from CFTC registration.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Ethereum news: Bitmine (BMNR) buys $92 million ETH as Tom Lee reaffirms 'crypto spring' call
items.subheadline: The largest Ethereum treasury firm slowed its buying pace but remains on track to reach its goal of owning 5% of ETH supply.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: OKX and NYSE partner to bridge Tradfi and crypto markets in joint venture led by Andrew Cuomo
items.subheadline: The goal is to enable OKX’s 120 million global users to access ICE futures and NYSE tokenized equities markets, the two U.S. companies announced.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Solana news: MoneyGram takes role validator role amid stablecoin payment push
items.subheadline: The remittances firm has become a validator on the Solana blockchain, helping process and secure transactions as it expands its stablecoin payments strategy.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Anchorage Digital aims to bring banks onchain with new tokenized deposit platform
items.subheadline: The crypto bank aims to help financial institutions offer round-the-clock payments and settlement without replacing their core systems.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Crypto's second U.S. lobbying front — tax policy — sees industry push on mining, staking
items.subheadline: The crypto sector's leading U.S. advocacy groups asked the U.S. House's tax committee to advance a bill to clarify treatment of assets from mining and staking.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: talent exodus sparks fresh debate over foundation leadership
items.subheadline: The Ethereum community is once again debating the future of the foundation after co-executive director Hsiao-Wei Wang announced she would step down from her leadership role, adding to a growing list of departures that have intensified scrutiny of the organization's management and direction.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum news: Joe Lubin, Sharplink, Tom Lee's Bitmine back new Ethereum research lab
items.subheadline: Ethlabs was formed with support from SharpLink, Bitmine and Consensys CEO Joe Lubin as the network's development is expanding beyond the Ethereum Foundation.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Strive says digital credit selloff was a liquidation event, not a credit crisis
items.subheadline: A sharp selloff in digital credit products exposed growing pains in a young market, a Strive executive argues the underlying credit fundamentals remain intact.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: 21Shares co-founder warns tokenization hype is outrunning Wall Street reality
items.subheadline: Tokenization can improve settlement and asset movement, but key pieces of financial infrastructure remain unprepared for institutional-scale adoption.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Ric Edelman says crypto’s biggest growth story is happening off the price chart
items.subheadline: Crypto prices remain under pressure, but Ric Edelman says institutional adoption and tokenization are accelerating behind the scenes.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Tokenization pioneers Securitize and tZERO clash over patents as Wall Street moves onchain
items.subheadline: tZERO and Securitize are squaring off over tokenization patents as the market attracts more of Wall Street's attention.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: U.S. Senate passes housing bill that carries four-year ban on a Fed CBDC
items.subheadline: The idea of a U.S. central bank digital currency — though little more than a research topic at the Federal Reserve — may be getting formally blocked.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Trump calls for powerful quantum computer
items.subheadline: The White House issued twin executive orders to accelerate U.S. development of large-scale quantum computers while simultaneously hardening defenses against such machines.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: ETH, SOL, DOGE price news: Bitcoin under $63,000 amid tech selloff
items.subheadline: A rotation out of this year's best AI and chip stocks sank Asian markets, with South Korea's Kospi down 6%, and crypto fell with them. Bitcoin is down more than 3% on the week.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: BTC analysis: SpaceX’s $600 billion plunge erased nearly half of bitcoin’s market cap in three days
items.subheadline: The newly public company shed close to half of bitcoin's entire market value in three sessions after announcing its first bond sale. Bitcoin, absorbing the same backdrop, fell less than 1%.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP drifts toward $1.10 support as traders await break from three-week range
items.subheadline: Weak volume and fading momentum kept XRP pinned near the bottom of its recent range, with the $1.05-$1.10 area emerging as the market's key line in the sand.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live BTC markets: An 'altcoin season' signal flashed, but bitcoin's slide is what set it off
items.subheadline: After nearly two years of declines, alts have run out of sellers and steadied, while bitcoin has dropped hard, sliding back toward $63,600.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
